turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE ROBERTS
turns.text: Justice Thomas has the opinion of the Court in two cases this morning.

turns.name: JUSTICE THOMAS
turns.text: The first case I have is Ohio Adjutant General's Department v. FLRA. This case comes to us on a writ of certiorari to the United States Court of Appeals for the Sixth Circuit. Petitioner asked us to determine whether the Federal Labor Relations Authority, the FLRA, properly exercised jurisdiction over an unfair labor practices dispute between the Ohio National Guard and the Union that represents the guards' federal employees known as dual-status technicians. Under the relevant federal statute, the FLRA has jurisdiction over federal agencies and the guard contends that it does not qualify as an agency under that statute. The FLRA and the Sixth Circuit rejected the guards' argument. In an opinion filed with the Court today, we affirm. We hold that the FLRA has jurisdiction over this labor dispute because a State National Guard acts as a federal agency for purposes of relevant federal law when it hires and supervises dual-status technicians serving in their civilian capacity. Dual-status technicians are unique class of federal civil service employees supervised by State National Guards but also statutorily defined and as federal civilian employees of the Department of the Army or the Department of the Air Force. In employing dual-status technicians in their civilian role, the guard exercises delegated federal authority from the Department of Army and Air Force. Those departments in turn are components of the Department of Defense, which is one of the federal agencies covered in the FLRA's jurisdictional statute. Because the guard exercises the authority of a covered agency in hiring and supervising employees of a covered agency, we conclude that it is subject to the FLRA's jurisdiction with respect to their employment. For these reasons and others set forth in our opinion, we affirm the judgment of the Sixth Circuit. Justice Alito has filed a dissenting opinion, in which Justice Gorsuch joins.
